page: Amazon Web Services c8a.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server features 96 physical cores and 96 vCPUs with a dedicated CPU allocation running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it features a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the c8a.metal-24xl in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance. In stress-ng and Geekbench testing, the server consistently achieves top 10% placement, supported by advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, vector neural network instructions, and bfloat16 support. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, with cached read-only small block transfers reaching 16,842.02 GB/sec and uncached large block writes at 192.59 GB/sec. Cryptographic and integer operations also rank in the top tier, as demonstrated by Passmark encryption and physics tests.

Due to its compute-heavy profile and high-speed memory throughput, this instance is highly efficient for workloads requiring intensive mathematical calculations without massive memory footprints. It excels in static web serving, database operations, software compilation, and large language model inference, where it achieves top-tier prompt processing and text generation speeds for models up to 70B parameters. The lack of bundled local storage means storage-heavy workloads must rely on network-attached options, which represents a key architectural tradeoff.
description: Amazon Web Services c8a.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by a 96-core AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture with a dedicated CPU allocation, it features 192.0 GB of system memory, translating to 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, but provides a 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data ranks this server in the top tier for single-core and multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic operations. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and bfloat16, facilitating rapid execution of complex mathematical tasks. The c8a.metal-24xl is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads, including software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and large language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring a 96-core AMD EPYC 9R45 CPU, 192 GB of RAM, and 40 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier performance for intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.metal-24xl is a 96-core bare-metal server with AMD EPYC processors, 192 GB RAM, and 40 Gbps network speed for compute-heavy tasks.
tagline: A bare-metal, ninety-six core compute-optimized platform delivering top-tier processor performance and high-speed memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 96-core AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz
Equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
Delivers 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Achieves top-tier benchmark results in multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth tests
Supports advanced vector and cryptographic instructions including AVX-512 and VAES
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and LLM inference
